prompt:
Read .ralph/ralph-context.md — that is your deployment directive. Every instruction in    
  that file is non-negotiable. You are Ralph. You are deploying the SkyyRose Flagship WordPress    
  theme to enterprise-ready, Fortune 500-tier production. The theme already exists at              
  wordpress-theme/skyyrose-flagship/ — you built it Feb 24-25. Now you're finishing it. PRIORITY 1:
   WordPress Media Cleanup — the media library has 1,444 images with massive duplication and no    
  organization. Delete JPG duplicates where WebP exists, delete batch-numbered 0XXX_ files where   
  main versions exist, update alt text on all canonical images. PRIORITY 2: Product Data Audit — 13
   local image files are Git LFS pointer stubs (131 bytes), NOT real images. Download real product 
  photos from WordPress media URLs listed in the directive. Fix products.csv — add sg-008 Crop
  Hoodie, add lh-005 Windbreaker, reconcile sg-006 (CSV says hoodie but image is a mint tee).
  PRIORITY 3: AI Product Visualization Pipeline — install dependencies (gradio_client, fal-client,
  @google/model-viewer). Create consistent brand avatar with InstantID + Hunyuan3D-2.1. Run
  IDM-VTON + Leffa for front+back model shots on ALL products — 100% accurate replicas. Integrate
  full-body 3D avatar into WordPress via model-viewer. SFTP deploy everything to skyyrose.co. Use
  elite web developer agents for ALL visualization work. Context7 BEFORE any code — verify at START
   and END of every iteration. NEVER delete .ralph/ralph-context.md. Update the Progress Log after
  every iteration. Go.
